text.title: Spatial UI Patterns: Agent Scene Scaffold Workflow and Gotchas
text.summary: Agent workflow for spatial UI design: subagent roles for panel-type mapping and scene generation, prompt patterns for platform-specific decisions, and LLM gotchas for XR code generation.

section.title: Agentic Workflow
section.p: Use agents to enumerate pattern fitness against device capabilities and to generate first-pass scene scaffolds. The panel-type decision is design-led; agents produce candidate matrices and emit platform-specific templates from the chosen pattern. Pair every agent suggestion with the platform HIG (visionOS HIG, Meta Horizon OS guidelines, MRTK design guides) — generic spatial advice causes most review failures.

rule.statement: Validate every agent-generated spatial layout against the platform HIG before implementing; generic XR patterns fail platform-specific review criteria.
rule.rationale: visionOS, Quest, and HoloLens have different scene models, ergonomic requirements, and review criteria. An agent trained on generic XR literature produces patterns that fail platform-specific submission review.

example.note: Panel mapping: "Given UI surfaces: [main menu, contextual tools, system alert, reference document], target platform visionOS 2, pose seated. Map each surface to a panel type from {world-locked, body-locked, hand-attached} and to the corresponding visionOS scene type (WindowGroup, Volume, ornament). Justify each choice against the visionOS HIG."

Ergonomic scoring: "Score this layout for ergonomic risk: panel A at 0.3 m world-locked, panel B head-locked overlay, panel C hand-attached left wrist. Criteria: arm-raise duration, FoV occupancy >40%, head-locked anti-pattern, occlusion. Emit a table with risk levels and fixes."

section.title: AI-Agent Gotchas

antipattern.description: Models confusing AR overlay (phone-screen) patterns with HMD (headset) patterns in the same response.
antipattern.reason: AR on a phone screen and AR in an HMD have fundamentally different ergonomic and interaction constraints. Prompts must specify the device class explicitly or the agent will mix incompatible patterns.

antipattern.description: Agent citing head-locked HUD as standard practice from older XR literature.
antipattern.reason: Pre-2023 XR literature, which dominates LLM training data, frequently recommends head-locked HUDs. Current Apple/Meta/Microsoft guidance explicitly prohibits or discourages this for non-telemetry use.

antipattern.description: Generated SwiftUI scenes mixing Window and ImmersiveSpace incorrectly — only one ImmersiveSpace can be active at a time in visionOS.
antipattern.reason: visionOS has a strict scene activation model. Multiple simultaneous ImmersiveSpaces crash the app. Agents unfamiliar with the platform's scene lifecycle make this error commonly.

antipattern.description: Coordinate system confusion: visionOS uses meters, but 3D assets often arrive in centimeters — agents drop one zero and place panels 10 m away.
antipattern.reason: Unit mismatch in 3D scenes produces panels at invisible distances or cramped inside the user's field of view. Always specify units explicitly and validate in simulator before hardware testing.

antipattern.description: Agent-generated spatial apps omitting Info.plist microphone/voice usage strings, blocking App Store review.
antipattern.reason: visionOS requires explicit usage description strings for microphone, camera, and hand-tracking access. Missing strings cause immediate App Store rejection; agents frequently omit them.

section.reference: Minimal visionOS scene scaffold with WindowGroup, Volume, and ImmersiveSpace
